meaning: n.勒索,勒索所得之款vt.勒索
examples: She started off by accusing him of blackmail but he more or less ignored her.
You were the one that brought up the idea of blackmail. I'd never be a party to such a thing.
We may leave our lights on in the house when we are on vacation, but through social media we tell criminals where we are, when we plan to return home, and how to blackmail us.
On the one hand, former Soviet Foreign Minister Eduard Shevardnadze engaged in what amounts to blackmail.
And blackmail usually works better when the practitioners are seen as irrational and unpredictable.
